mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the next week as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, which compresses profit margins and generates negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.0 percent, markets price in aggressive future rate hikes, causing immediate negative short-term equity returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment alongside falling unemployment confirms a healthy labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and yields positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts signal increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which flows through to corporate revenues and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average indicates a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and triggers negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of periods when INDPRO drops below its 12-month average.
